svg.text: And if k isn't integer ? For k = 0.5, we can find interesting fomula : cos ( 2 * A ) = cos2 ( A ) - sin2 ( A ) As cos2 ( A ) + sin2 ( A ) = 1 cos ( 2 * A ) = ( cos2 ( A ) - sin2 ( A ) ) / ( cos2 ( A ) + sin2 ( A ) ) We divise all terms by cos2 ( A ) cos ( 2 * A ) = ( 1 - tan2 ( A ) ) / ( 1 + tan2 ( A ) ) We use t = tan ( A / 2 ) cos ( A ) = ( 1 - t2 ) / ( 1 + t2 ) For sinus, we use same method sin ( 2 * A ) = 2 * sin ( A ) * cos ( A ) sin ( 2 * A ) = 2 * sin ( A ) * cos ( A ) / ( cos2 ( A ) + sin2 ( A ) ) sin ( 2 * A ) = 2 * tan ( A ) / ( 1 + tan2 ( A ) ) sin ( A ) = 2 * t / ( 1 + t2 ) For tangente we have tan ( 2 * A ) = 2 * tan ( A ) / ( 1 - tan2 ( A ) ) tan ( A ) = 2 * t / ( 1 - t2 ) We can replace sin ( A ), cos ( A ) and tan ( A ) by single variable t in equation Of course, we multiply degree for equation by two ...
